page: Amazon Web Services m6i.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing the Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates consistent mid-tier performance across multiple workloads. The instance achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 1628.0 and a multi-core score of 13858.0, alongside a Passmark CPU score of 36442.6. In memory-intensive operations, it recorded a read-only large block memory bandwidth of 143.04 GB/sec and a write large block bandwidth of 67.37 GB/sec. Database operations via Redis SET reached 31.22 million operations per second, while static web serving throughput was measured at 34.89 GB/sec.

While the instance demonstrates strong performance in text generation for small LLM models at 302.4 tokens per second, it exhibits significant limitations with larger models. Large model text generation drops to a poor 2.25 tokens per second, highlighting a clear tradeoff for complex AI workloads. Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the instance is suited for general-purpose workloads such as web serving, database operations, software compilation, and moderate-scale data processing that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services m6i.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory, delivering a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Benchmark results place the server in the average tier for most tasks, with a Passmark CPU score of 36442.6 and a Redis SET throughput of 31.22 million operations per second. While it handles small LLM text generation efficiently at 302.4 tokens per second, performance drops significantly on larger models, with 70B text generation falling to 2.25 tokens per second. This instance is suited for web serving, database operations, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6i.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 32 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8375C CPU, delivering balanced performance for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6i.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3.5 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, DDR4 memory, and reliable mid-tier performance for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 32-vCPU Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz
Equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory with a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
Delivers 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Achieves 31.22 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
Suited for web serving, database operations, and software compilation
